promotion_tool: governed dev→release promotion (copy allow-listed changed files + ZIP)
store_upload: NONE — no Chrome Web Store upload performed by this promotion
github_release: NONE — no GitHub Release created by this promotion
note: Governed dev→release promotion of AG-PROMPT-389 (canonical Ai Notice icon integration) on top of the AG-387 baseline (finance report false-negative fix), which itself sat on the AG-384/AG-383/AG-382/AG-381/AG-380/AG-379/AG-378 lineage — none of those are pending, they remain shipped and unchanged in this release. Exactly five allow-listed files changed since the AG-387 promotion (source commit 3b38c9e): public/icons/icon16.png, public/icons/icon32.png, public/icons/icon48.png, public/icons/icon128.png, and package.json (adds one new npm script entry, 'test:icon-integrity', pointing at a dev-only test script that is intentionally NOT on the release allow-list; this script is not invoked by build:chrome, package:chrome, or release-verify.ts, so it has no effect on the shipped artifact — flagged as a follow-up in the AG-390 report, not fixed here as it would require an allow-list change, which is out of scope for an icon-only promotion). Confirmed via git diff between dev commits 3b38c9e and a88a74f restricted to the release-export.ts allow-list, and independently via a full recursive diff (CRLF-normalized) between a fresh release-export.ts dry-run of dev HEAD and this release repo's pre-promotion working tree — which additionally surfaced two PRE-EXISTING, unrelated allow-listed files (docs/CHROME-SUBMISSION-CHECKLIST.md, scripts/test-chrome-build-artifacts.ts) that were already stale in this release repo before AG-390 (predating source commit 3b38c9e, i.e. a gap left by an earlier promotion, not caused by AG-389 or AG-390). Both are repo-tracked-only (not part of dist/chrome or the shipped ZIP) and were deliberately left untouched to preserve AG-390's icon-only scope; recorded as a follow-up for a future dedicated prompt. AG-389 root cause and fix (icon replacement only, no detection/policy/UI change): all four old packaged icons were flat single-color placeholders (icon16.png and icon48.png were byte-identical — icon48 was mislabeled 16x16 art, not real 48px art); replaced byte-for-byte (no re-render/recompress/resize) with the canonical Ai Notice mark derivatives formalized in AG-388, copied from the authoritative website repository (https://github.com/drk345/agentguard-site.git, commit efdba3b82a4c1846d888e6097f960ae1383003b7, canonical SVG assets/brand/ainotice-mark.svg sha256 f0fae58dab9d24cbcb92a551929405a577c64a2aa434796a7ae0d839fe0ef76b). Approved hashes verified byte-exact at every stage: dev source (public/icons/), dev Chrome build output (dist/chrome/icons/), dev Firefox build output (dist/firefox/icons/), release working tree (public/icons/), and the final packaged ZIP (release/ainotice-chrome.zip) — icon16 e562af70..., icon32 02cf5879..., icon48 6fad62a2..., icon128 5a3f08a1..., all matching exactly, all confirmed NOT to match any of the three known placeholder hashes. Version decision: REUSED 1.0.0 (no manifest or package.json version field changed) — package.json/manifest.chrome.json/manifest.firefox.json have shown 1.0.0 unchanged across the full recorded git history spanning multiple prior promotions (AG-380 through AG-387), and no Chrome Web Store upload has ever been performed for this extension (every prior promotion note confirms 'no store upload'), so there is no draft-version conflict to avoid. During promotion, a pre-existing environmental defect was found and corrected: this fresh release-ag390 clone had no .gitattributes (unlike the dev repo, which enforces eol=lf) and this machine's global git core.autocrlf=true caused git to check out all text files with CRLF line endings on initial clone — git's own 'status' masked this as clean (autocrlf-aware comparison), but it caused the first release-side build:chrome + package:chrome run to produce a ZIP differing from the dev staging ZIP in 5 unrelated static files (manifest.json, popup.html, warning-modal.css, warning-modal.html, messages.json byte content, NOT content.js/background.js which are minified and CRLF-insensitive). Root-caused, corrected via local git config (core.autocrlf=false; core.eol=lf) and a full re-checkout restoring the canonical LF-committed bytes (git blobs/history untouched, working-tree-only fix), then reproduced: the corrected release-side ZIP is now byte-identical (matching SHA-256) to the dev-side staging ZIP built from the same dev HEAD. Dev-side verification: npm ci, tsc --noEmit clean, icon-integrity test 42/42 PASS (source) then 66/66 PASS (source + Chrome + Firefox build output, cross-checked independently via raw sha256sum outside the test script), CORE unit suite 56/56, Chrome MV3 build 34/34 artifact checks + 20/20 consumer-modal-copy guard, Firefox MV2 build 25/25 artifact checks, verify:manifest 28/28, taxonomy-consistency 8 checks/0 FAIL/2 pre-existing unrelated WARN (national-ID signal registry, not touched by this promotion). Release-side verification: release-verify.ts 10/10 gates (type-check, build:chrome, package:chrome, license UX guardrail, no-activity-record, message-handler safety, storage manager, storage hardening, signal-manifest integrity, manifest verification). Final ZIP compared file-by-file against the superseded ZIP: exactly and only the 4 icon files differ; content.js, background.js, manifest.json (byte-identical — confirms zero permission/host/CSP/version drift), popup.html, warning-modal.css, warning-modal.html, and _locales/en/messages.json are all byte-identical to the superseded release; file count unchanged (11 files / 14 ZIP entries including directory entries, both before and after). Host permissions confirmed unchanged: permissions=['storage'] only (no activeTab), host_permissions exactly the 6 listed AI site patterns, no wildcard/<all_urls>, connect-src 'none'. No Tier-0/RiskSignal/dedup/chunking/detection/policy/ScanScheduler/destination-detection change — this promotion touches icon assets and release packaging only. Original repositories C:\dev\agentguard, C:\dev\ainotice-release, and C:\dev\agentguard-site were not modified (read-only inspection only). No Chrome Web Store upload; no GitHub Release; package status is READY_FOR_OPERATOR_SMOKE pending manual operator verification.
